\medskip\noindent\textbf{Self-application.}~\textnormal{(specialized to call-by-need)}\quad $\lambda x.\, x\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
def _program():
    v_1 = CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL

    def v_0():
        nonlocal v_1
        if v_1 is CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL:

            def v_2(v_3):
                v_1_2 = CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL

                def v_0_2():
                    nonlocal v_1_2
                    if v_1_2 is CALL_BY_NEED_SENTINEL:
                        v_1_2 = v_3()(v_3)()
                    return v_1_2
                return v_0_2
            v_1 = v_2
        return v_1
    return v_0
program = _program()
\end{lstlisting}

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Local specialization in a cyclic term.}~\textnormal{(specialized to interpret)}\quad $Y\,(\mathtt{cons}\,(3 \times 3))$
The cyclic stream is fold-requiring, so the whole term stays interpreted; its closed element $3 \times 3$ is simply typable, hence a call-by-value island compiled to strict Python:
\begin{lstlisting}
(lambda v0: lambda v1: lambda v2: v0(v1(v2)))(lambda v0: lambda v1: v0(v0(v0(v1))))(lambda v0: lambda v1: v0(v0(v0(v1))))
\end{lstlisting}
The island runs and the interpreter folds around it, so the result is the same finite cyclic behaviour as pure interpretation, the stream $\mathtt{cons}\,9\,(\mathtt{cons}\,9\,\cdots)$ folded to a single cyclic node.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{The compiler, specialized: finding call-by-value islands.}\quad The compiler is untypable as a whole, since its fixpoint combinator $Z$ self-applies, so it stays interpreted; the specializer finds its 5 maximal closed simply-typable sub-terms, each a strongly-normalizing combinator compiled to a strict call-by-value island, while the recursive skeleton is left to the interpreter:
\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, \lambda w.\, \lambda u.\, \lambda s.\, u\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v0: lambda v1: lambda v2: lambda v3: lambda v4: lambda v5: v4(v0)
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v0: v0
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, y\, (\lambda z.\, \lambda w.\, \lambda u.\, z\, (\lambda s.\, \lambda t.\, t\, (s\, w))\, (\lambda s.\, u)\, (\lambda s.\, s))\, x$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v0: lambda v1: v1(lambda v2: lambda v3: lambda v4: v2(lambda v5: lambda v6: v6(v5(v3)))(lambda v5: v4)(lambda v5: v5))(v0)
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, y\, (x\, y\, z)$
\begin{lstlisting}
lambda v0: lambda v1: lambda v2: v1(v0(v1)(v2))
\end{lstlisting}

\smallskip\noindent $\lambda x.\, \lambda y.\, \lambda z.\, \lambda w.\, \lambda u.\, \lambda s.\, s\, x$

\end{lstlisting}
